meta.paper: On the Necessary Substructures of Finite Contact Graphs
meta.suite: concrete contact-graph numerics; ground premise (floor from infinitude), T0-T8, and measurement (self-defeat, falsification); randomized sweep + standalone witnesses

standalone_witnesses.name: T2_region_not_point
standalone_witnesses.claim: Residual is realised by a multi-vertex bipartition, not a single vertex: truth is a region, never a point.

results.name: ground_floor_from_infinitude
results.claim: Floor from Infinitude: every thing (proper part) has a strictly positive identification residual >= floor; the comparison against the rest never completes (no zero cut).

results.name: T0_floor
results.claim: Floor Theorem: every nonempty proper part has cut weight >= floor > 0; no sharp separation.

results.name: T1_negation
results.claim: Individuation by negation: a part is fixed as the complement of its negation-set; complementation is a selector-free involution.

results.name: T2T3_residual
results.claim: Residual is bounded below by the floor and invariant under reshuffling (weighted isomorphism). It is the conserved quantity beneath the relabellable surface.

results.name: T4_private
results.claim: Every part carries a private invariant (its boundary cost to the medium), positive and bounded below by the floor.

results.name: T5_gate
results.claim: Selection requires a gate: branching vertices (degree >= 2) leave the next step underdetermined by the graph alone; reachability is near-total so the gate fixes the order.

results.name: T6_nonreturn
results.claim: Committed count strictly monotone; revisiting a vertex is not returning to a state; an undo increments the count; a growing complement distinguishes a thing from its earlier stage.

results.name: T7_quotient
results.claim: Quotient inherits a positive floor; a coherent collective is single-gated (the collective gate is a single-valued selector over classes).

results.name: T8_authored
results.claim: Authored structures exist (specify-then-instantiate yields a contact graph); bounded by open-endedness (forward closure states all distinct), non-duplication (copy differs in committed count), floor-limited authorship (no sub-floor edge).

results.name: measurement
results.claim: Measurement = reshuffle conserves the invariant residual and the medium; identity (interior residue / separation) never reaches a point (self-defeat); resolution does not saturate.

results.name: ground_floor_from_infinitude
results.claim: Floor from Infinitude: every thing (proper part) has a strictly positive identification residual >= floor; the comparison against the rest never completes (no zero cut).

results.name: T0_floor
results.claim: Floor Theorem: every nonempty proper part has cut weight >= floor > 0; no sharp separation.

results.name: T1_negation
results.claim: Individuation by negation: a part is fixed as the complement of its negation-set; complementation is a selector-free involution.

results.name: T2T3_residual
results.claim: Residual is bounded below by the floor and invariant under reshuffling (weighted isomorphism). It is the conserved quantity beneath the relabellable surface.

results.name: T4_private
results.claim: Every part carries a private invariant (its boundary cost to the medium), positive and bounded below by the floor.

results.name: T5_gate
results.claim: Selection requires a gate: branching vertices (degree >= 2) leave the next step underdetermined by the graph alone; reachability is near-total so the gate fixes the order.

results.name: T6_nonreturn
results.claim: Committed count strictly monotone; revisiting a vertex is not returning to a state; an undo increments the count; a growing complement distinguishes a thing from its earlier stage.

results.name: T7_quotient
results.claim: Quotient inherits a positive floor; a coherent collective is single-gated (the collective gate is a single-valued selector over classes).

results.name: T8_authored
results.claim: Authored structures exist (specify-then-instantiate yields a contact graph); bounded by open-endedness (forward closure states all distinct), non-duplication (copy differs in committed count), floor-limited authorship (no sub-floor edge).

results.name: ground_floor_from_infinitude
results.claim: Floor from Infinitude: every thing (proper part) has a strictly positive identification residual >= floor; the comparison against the rest never completes (no zero cut).

results.name: T0_floor
results.claim: Floor Theorem: every nonempty proper part has cut weight >= floor > 0; no sharp separation.

results.name: T1_negation
results.claim: Individuation by negation: a part is fixed as the complement of its negation-set; complementation is a selector-free involution.

results.name: T2T3_residual
results.claim: Residual is bounded below by the floor and invariant under reshuffling (weighted isomorphism). It is the conserved quantity beneath the relabellable surface.

results.name: T4_private
results.claim: Every part carries a private invariant (its boundary cost to the medium), positive and bounded below by the floor.

results.name: T5_gate
results.claim: Selection requires a gate: branching vertices (degree >= 2) leave the next step underdetermined by the graph alone; reachability is near-total so the gate fixes the order.

results.name: T6_nonreturn
results.claim: Committed count strictly monotone; revisiting a vertex is not returning to a state; an undo increments the count; a growing complement distinguishes a thing from its earlier stage.

results.name: T7_quotient
results.claim: Quotient inherits a positive floor; a coherent collective is single-gated (the collective gate is a single-valued selector over classes).

results.name: T8_authored
results.claim: Authored structures exist (specify-then-instantiate yields a contact graph); bounded by open-endedness (forward closure states all distinct), non-duplication (copy differs in committed count), floor-limited authorship (no sub-floor edge).

results.name: ground_floor_from_infinitude
results.claim: Floor from Infinitude: every thing (proper part) has a strictly positive identification residual >= floor; the comparison against the rest never completes (no zero cut).

results.name: T0_floor
results.claim: Floor Theorem: every nonempty proper part has cut weight >= floor > 0; no sharp separation.

results.name: T1_negation
results.claim: Individuation by negation: a part is fixed as the complement of its negation-set; complementation is a selector-free involution.

results.name: T2T3_residual
results.claim: Residual is bounded below by the floor and invariant under reshuffling (weighted isomorphism). It is the conserved quantity beneath the relabellable surface.

results.name: T4_private
results.claim: Every part carries a private invariant (its boundary cost to the medium), positive and bounded below by the floor.

results.name: T5_gate
results.claim: Selection requires a gate: branching vertices (degree >= 2) leave the next step underdetermined by the graph alone; reachability is near-total so the gate fixes the order.

results.name: T6_nonreturn
results.claim: Committed count strictly monotone; revisiting a vertex is not returning to a state; an undo increments the count; a growing complement distinguishes a thing from its earlier stage.

results.name: T7_quotient
results.claim: Quotient inherits a positive floor; a coherent collective is single-gated (the collective gate is a single-valued selector over classes).

results.name: T8_authored
results.claim: Authored structures exist (specify-then-instantiate yields a contact graph); bounded by open-endedness (forward closure states all distinct), non-duplication (copy differs in committed count), floor-limited authorship (no sub-floor edge).
